title: buphthalmum salicifolium subsp. grandiflorum
intro: Buphthalmum salicifolium subsp. grandiflorum, also known as the large-flowered bur-marigold, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Buphthalmum salicifolium subsp. grandiflorum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. The herbal preparations of Buphthalmum salicifolium subsp. grandiflorum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
